prevalence.value: high seroprevalence in many settings; outbreaks occur in susceptible urban groups
prevalence.trend: shifting epidemiology with improving sanitation: fewer childhood infections, more adult outbreaks in some cities

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; higher in adult outbreaks
burden.mortalityRate: Low; severe disease in adults is uncommon but can occur

vulnerablePopulations: unvaccinated adolescents and adults in higher-income urban groups
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute hepatitis with anti-HAV IgM positivity; clinical syndrome and elevated transaminases

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; outbreak detection and testing of symptomatic cases
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High recovery; prevention focuses on sanitation and vaccination in specific risk settings

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Serology (anti-HAV IgM) used in outbreak investigations and hospital cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; public health response to water/food source contamination
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Waterborne outbreaks and uneven access to clean water/sanitation across states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Public health outbreak response capacity through NCDC and state surveillance systems
